methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Prusa Research is a bootstrapped frontier-tech company operating in Robotics & Manufacturing. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers total: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set presents material limitations for valuation.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The sector contains 11 total Robotics & Manufacturing peers, but filtering by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier yields fewer than 3 datapoints in any narrow cohort. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable comparability bias, as stage, maturity, and business model divergence across the broader peer set obscure meaningful value signals specific to Prusa's position.

Given the absence of a sufficiently granular comparable set and the target's bootstrapped status, traditional market multiples cannot reliably triangulate valuation. Alternative approaches—including discounted cash flow analysis, cost-of-capital assessment, or narrative anchoring to the identified private peers—would require operational and financial data not provided in this dataset. The valuation analysis is therefore inconclusive pending either: (1) identification of same-stage robotics peers with disclosed valuations, or (2) access to Prusa's revenue, EBITDA, or growth trajectory for DCF modeling.
